PaymentVoucher.title: PaymentVoucher
PaymentVoucher.description: Represents a single payment voucher transaction in the Tropical Ledger system, capturing details for financial record-keeping and voucher generation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PaymentVoucher entity.

voucherNumber.description: The unique number assigned to the payment voucher, serving as its primary reference.

dateIssued.description: The date and time when the payment voucher was officially issued.

paidToRecipient.description: The full name or entity name of the recipient to whom the payment is being made.

amountRialOmani.description: The whole number part of the payment amount in Rial Omani (R.O.).

amountBaisa.description: The fractional part of the payment amount in Baisa (Bz), the sub-unit of Rial Omani (1 R.O. = 1000 Bz). This value typically ranges from 0 to 999.

totalAmount.description: The combined total amount of the voucher in Rial Omani, represented as a decimal number (e.g., 10.500 R.O.), calculated from amountRialOmani and amountBaisa.

amountInWords.description: The textual representation of the total amount in Rial Omani (e.g., 'Ten Rial Omani and Five Hundred Baisa').

paymentMethod.description: The method used for making the payment (e.g., 'Cash', 'Cheque', 'Bank Transfer').

bankName.description: The name of the bank involved in the transaction, applicable if the payment method is 'Cheque' or 'Bank Transfer'.

referenceNumber.description: The specific reference number, cheque number, or transaction ID for the payment, applicable if the payment method is 'Cheque' or 'Bank Transfer'.

purpose.description: A brief description explaining the reason or purpose for the payment ('Being').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this voucher record was initially created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this voucher record was last modified or updated.

definition.description: Stores individual payment voucher records. All documents in this collection are accessible by any authenticated user who is authorized to use the 'Tropical Ledger' system. Authorization is checked against the '/roles_ledger_access' collection.

params.name: voucherId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific PaymentVoucher document.

definition.description: This collection defines which users are authorized to access the 'Tropical Ledger' system. The existence of a document at `/roles_ledger_access/{userId}` signifies that `userId` is a 'trusted person' with general access rights to the system's data (e.g., payment vouchers). This implements Database Access Control (DBAC) by storing roles in the database.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of a user authorized to access the system.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for 'Tropical Ledger' prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability in line with the core design principles. Given that the system is designed to digitize a company's internal voucher process, the assumption is that all authorized users (employees) within the 'Tropical Ledger' system will have access to the *entire* collection of payment vouchers. This leads to a flattened, top-level collection for the `PaymentVoucher` entities.

**Authorization Independence:** The `PaymentVoucher` documents are stored in a top-level collection `/paymentVouchers`. Each voucher document is self-contained, meaning its authorization does not depend on data from a parent document (e.g., a 'Project' or 'Department' document). This eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in security rules to check parent document attributes, thus supporting atomic operations and greatly simplifying rule debugging. The authorization for accessing any `PaymentVoucher` document is determined solely by whether the `request.auth.uid` is present in the `/roles_ledger_access` collection, indicating they are a 'trusted person' for the system.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** This structure inherently supports secure `list` operations. Because all documents within the `/paymentVouchers` collection share the same security posture (any authorized user can view/manage any voucher), a `list` query will never return documents that an authorized user is not permitted to see. Firestore security rules will enforce that only authenticated users whose `uid` exists in the `/roles_ledger_access` collection can read (and write) from `/paymentVouchers`. This ensures that `list` operations are efficient and do not rely on filtering data on the client side or complex, slow `get()` calls within rules for each item in a list.
